comments.text: Amid all the other plane crash-related news this weekend, a skydiving plane crash in Missouri killed all 12 aboard. It is tied with a crash in 1995 for the deadliest skydiving plane crash in US history and the worst US skydiving related accident since the 1967 Lake Erie skydiving disaster.
These men are trapped at Sugfussion Camp Sandy Lake Ontario: We need emergency assistance 🆘 🆘 🆘

Before I check out my newly acquired plane it is important to note the 3 humans were prepared and had LIFE JACKETS on! Well done pilot and USCG Great Lakes group

In 2004, explorers began a search for a plane that crashed into Lake Michigan in 1950, killing 58 people. In the process, they revived the history of what once was the U.S.'s deadliest aviation accident.

Two guys needed to be rescued from thin ice after falling in while ice fishing about 3 miles south of me on Saturday. Delta County sheriff’s office airboat was used to rescue them. www.dailypress.net/news/local-n...
The U.S. Coast Guard rescued five people suffering from hypothermia and seasickness after a boat was reported to be taking on water in Lake Michigan.

For the five of you that care, the Secret Nerd Podcast canon for The Corrupted is that the plane crashed on Apr. 6, 2024 in Lake Erie (with other pieces falling off across New England) and outbreak day followed 5 days later on the 11th.
All 3 people were rescued safely from the plane that crashed in Lake Michigan a couple hours ago.

Lake Michigan — 75 years ago today “Northwest Airlines flight 2501 disappeared over Lake Michigan. Flying from New York to Seattle, 58 passengers and crew were lost over the lake.” — Wisconsin Historical Society Learn more here: www.michiganshipwrecks.org/shipwrecks-2...

🧵 1/9 #OTD in 1950: Northwest Orient Airlines 2501, a DC-4 with 58 aboard, disappears in Lake Michigan (US) - worst US accident until then. Main wreck was never located. Cause undetermined, likely crew inadvertently crossed bad weather, not having received updated forecasts.

42 years ago today – September 10, 1983 A remarkable emergency landing took place in the GTA when pilot Bruce MacRitchie lost the propeller of his 1937 Fleet 21K biplane while flying over Lake Ontario. #OnThisDay #OTD #aircraft #1980s #AvGeek #Toronto #Scarborough #canada #jeremyhopkin
A small plane crashed into Lake Michigan near Wind Point, Wisconsin. The Coast Guard rescued all three passengers, who were wearing life jackets.

June 25, 1950 After a day-long search by 60 planes, 13 lake vessels, a helicopter, and a diver, Northwest Airlines officials last night gave up hope for a DC4 plane which is feared to have plunged into Lake Michigan. The four-engined plane was carrying 55 passengers and a crew of three from New York

A Lufthansa Boeing 747-8 (D-ABYH) operating LH431 from Chicago O’Hare to Frankfurt returned to O’Hare shortly after takeoff when the crew discovered the main landing gear failed to retract. The crew halted the climb at about 6,000 ft, held over Lake Michigan to dump fuel.

A Cirrus went down in Lake Ontario off Tommy Thompson Park today. The CAPS system was deployed. Minor injuries to the pilot who was pulled from the water by emergency crews. www.cp24.com/news/2025/05... #aviation

In January 1971 a B-52 aircraft was on a training mission, somehow lost control, and crashed into Lake Michigan. Mysteriously, very little of the aircraft remained, and the bodies of the 9-member crew were never found.
